channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: An ultrafast plenoptic-camera system for high-resolution 3D particle tracking in unsegmented scintillators
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70918-x

We propose an alternative approach to elementary particle detection: a plenoptic system combined with time-resolving single-photon avalanche diode array sensors leads to ultrafast three-dimensional high-resolution imaging in unsegmented scintillator.
item.dc:title: An ultrafast plenoptic-camera system for high-resolution 3D particle tracking in unsegmented scintillators
item.dc:creator: Till Dieminger
Saúl Alonso-Monsalve
Christoph Alt
Claudio Bruschini
Noemi Bührer
Edoardo Charbon
Kodai Kaneyasu
Tim Weber
Matthew Franks
Davide Sgalaberna

item.title: Supercurrent-controlled spontaneous ferromagnetism of magnetic impurities in a spin-orbit-coupled superconductor
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70968-1

In conventional s-wave superconductors, ferromagnetism is strongly suppressed, due to the singlet pairing of the electrons. Here, Xiang, He, Lin, and coauthors find a long-range ferromagnetic order among impurity sites in the spin-orbit coupled Fe(Se,Te) using scanning superconducting quantum interference device microscopy.
item.dc:title: Supercurrent-controlled spontaneous ferromagnetism of magnetic impurities in a spin-orbit-coupled superconductor
item.dc:creator: Bingke Xiang
Qiushi He
Yishi Lin
Keyi Liang
Zhijie Li
Jinjiang Zhu
Boren Chen
Youfang Wang
Puhua Zhang
Hongxu Yao
Chunxiang Wu
Tingyu Zhou
Minghu Fang
Yao Lu
Ilya Tokatly
Sebastian Bergeret
Yihua Wang

item.title: Proton transfer regulated photocured robust room-temperature phosphorescence from naphthalimide
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 21 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70999-8

Photocured materials with room temperature phosphorescence are potentially useful but can be challenging to prepare. Here, the authors report the development of such materials by use of naphthalimide as both a chromophore and photoinitiator.
item.dc:title: Proton transfer regulated photocured robust room-temperature phosphorescence from naphthalimide
item.dc:creator: Aicheng Wang
Haoxuan Wei
Kunquan Lin
Xing Huang
Mingxing Chen
Wentao Bian
Junxiao Wang
Yuzhou Qiao
Bing Fang
Yuxia Zhao
Jianxiang Yu
Meizhen Yin
Yuhua Dai
